economic_notes: Investments in Aseneskak Casino Limited Partnership (16.67%), Aseneskak Company Inc. (16.67%), CCN-Arnason Industries Ltd. Joint Venture (51%), Nekote Limited Partnership (14.29%), Nekote General Partner Inc. (14.29%), Mahihkan Bus Lines Limited Partnership (16.67%), Mahihkan Bus Lines General Partner Inc. (16.67%). Operations include gaming, forestry, bus lines, construction, real estate.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2020. Financial statements are consolidated. 12 subsidiaries fully consolidated including CCN School Inc., Chemawawin Health Authority, Chemawawin Housing Authority, Gaming Commission, and others. 7 entities accounted for by modified equity method including Aseneskak Casino LP, Mahihkan Bus Lines LP, Nekote LP, and related general partners. Net financial assets per Statement of Change in Net Financial Assets = 5,522,216. Long-term debt detail not fully extracted from available text. Deferred revenue balance not fully captured due to truncated notes. Accumulated remeasurement gains at year end = 464,922. Manitoba Hydro revenue significantly lower than prior year (4,633,888 in 2019 vs 1,739,265 in 2020). Total government transfers calculated as ISC + Health Canada + CMHC + HRSDC + Province of Manitoba + Frontier School Division = 18,550,965 + 3,610,439 + 608,979 + 518,286 + 534,713 + 316,330 = 24,139,712; OSR = 29,124,754 - 24,139,712 = 4,985,042. Loss on disposal of capital assets of 235,605 recorded as other expense.
